title: Codonoboea humilis
intro: Codonoboea humilis, also known as "Malaysian Cat's Claw Vine," is a tropical vine native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its climbing habit and small, green leaves. The health benefits of Codonoboea humilis include its potential in treating various inflammatory conditions, such as rheumatism and arthritis, due to its anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoid glycosides and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Codonoboea humilis is used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Codonoboea humilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It belongs to the genus Codonoboea. Codonoboea humilis is native to New Guinea and neighboring islands. The plant is an epiphyte, which means it grows on other plants for support. It is a relatively small plant with delicate, green foliage and small, white flowers.
